title: Multi-Bank Payout Orchestration
subtitle: Cost-aware routing with health-based failover
challenges: Selecting cheapest healthy rail in real-time under fluctuating bank health
Enforcing per-bank throughput contracts without dropping traffic
Handling partner downtime without impacting transaction flow
decisions: Sliding-window rate limiter per bank to enforce SLA boundaries
Dynamic cost matrix recalculated on health and latency signals
Priority-based failover with circuit breaker guards
failureHandling: Automatic failover to secondary banks on timeout/error thresholds
Retry pipeline for transient bank failures
outcome: Sustained high-throughput payouts with sub-second routing decisions and no single point of failure

title: Concurrent Wallet with Distributed Locking
subtitle: Consistency-first balance management under contention
challenges: Concurrent debit/credit operations on the same wallet
Stale writes due to network delays or retries
decisions: Redis SETNX with TTL for distributed locking
Fencing tokens to prevent stale writes
PostgreSQL as source of truth with transactional guarantees
failureHandling: TTL-based lock expiry for crash recovery
outcome: Strong consistency guarantees with safe concurrent transaction handling under load

title: Async Retry & Dead-Letter Pipeline
subtitle: Reliable transaction state resolution
challenges: Handling asynchronous bank responses without blocking flows
decisions: Exponential backoff strategy with capped retries
failureHandling: Retry with increasing delay for transient errors
outcome: Near-complete transaction resolution with no long-lived in-flight states

title: Sliding-Window Rate Limiter
subtitle: Precise throughput control across banks and merchants
challenges: Strict bank TPS enforcement without burst violations
decisions: Sliding-window log using Redis sorted sets
outcome: Consistent SLA adherence with controlled traffic shaping

title: Liquidity & Prefunding Distribution
subtitle: Dynamic capital allocation across banking partners
challenges: Balancing idle capital vs transaction failure risk
Adapting to real-time bank performance changes
Preventing fund exhaustion during traffic spikes
decisions: Weighted allocation using TPS, success rate, and cost
Continuous rebalancing based on utilization signals
outcome: Optimized capital utilization with stable transaction success rates

title: Idempotent Payment Processing Layer
subtitle: Exactly-once semantics over unreliable networks
challenges: Duplicate requests from retries or client errors
Network timeouts leading to uncertain transaction states
decisions: Idempotency keys with request fingerprint validation
Cached response replay for duplicate requests
failureHandling: Fallback to reconciliation for uncertain states
outcome: Exactly-once transaction guarantees across retries and failures
